# Epic: Add Club & Activity Management Features

This epic collects features identified when comparing the Mergington API to the FOSS@Amrita club website. Create separate issues from this checklist or use it as a single tracking issue.

## Missing features (checklist)

- [ ] Membership & Registration: admin review/approval workflow, full user profiles, mentor flag, profile fields (bio, typing speed, year)
- [ ] Social OAuth: login/registration via social networks
- [ ] Promotion / CMS pages: promotional templates, application emails and Telegram integration
- [ ] Rich Events/Workshops: trainer bio, level, prerequisites, travel, accommodation, expense, lab requirements, approval/publish flags
- [ ] Project pages: project listings, project detail pages, mentor/mentee relationships, images
- [ ] Achievements module: articles, internships, talks, contest/GSoC timelines
- [ ] Documents management: file uploads, document catalog, per-user access and update forms
- [ ] Technical resources library: categorized links and uploaded files per category
- [ ] Attendance logging & reporting: client integration, attendance percentage reports, admin views
- [ ] Status updates / mailing-list integration: weekly status updates and mailing list configuration
- [ ] User-submitted content & moderation: review/feedback forms and content moderation flows
- [ ] Admin UI & permissions: admin-driven content creation/verification and permission model
- [ ] REST API & Auth: authenticated API (JWT) and extended endpoints (status, attendance, documents)
- [ ] Rich form UX & editors: TinyMCE or other rich-text editors, datepickers, enhanced widgets
- [ ] Persistent DB & media handling: migration to a persistent DB (MySQL/Postgres) and media storage
- [ ] Install & ops docs: extended install and deployment documentation (DB setup, migrations)
- [ ] Timeline / historical UI components: timeline pages and advanced frontend components

---

If you want, I can also:
- Create individual GitHub issues for every checklist item.
- Open a single GitHub Issue (not a file) instead — I can do that if you prefer.
